rag_rules: Emit only valid AGREE constructs inside an annex agree block.
Reference only identifiers that are visible in the owning component scope and match the model exactly.
Treat each annex agree block as scoped to its owning component declaration.
Use the AADL model as the authority for available names, ports, directions, types, subcomponents, and connections.
generic_rules: Prefer contracts over structural restatement unless the requirement can be expressed with visible symbols.
Do not encode AADL property associations as AGREE expressions unless explicit typed model symbols expose them.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Valid AGREE forms include assume, guarantee, eq, const, and assign statements with terminating semicolons.
Assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations are typically written in component type annexes; assignments are implementation-level when valid in scope.
anti_patterns: Do not invent identifiers, copy example names, or reference symbols not confirmed by the model.
Do not treat AADL property names or unit tokens as automatically available AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: Validator-driven repair steps are not relevant because no diagnostics are active.
Model insertion-location decisions are outside this agent's digest.
